title: Amor Intellectualis
content:
Oft have we trod the vales of Castaly
    And heard sweet notes of sylvan music blown
    From antique reeds to common folk unknown:
    And often launched our bark upon that sea
    Which the nine Muses hold in empery,
    And ploughed free furrows through the wave and foam,
    Nor spread reluctant sail for more safe home
    Till we had freighted well our argosy.
    Of which despoiled treasures these remain,
    Sordello's passion, and the honeyed line
    Of young Endymion, lordly Tamburlaine
    Driving his pampered jades, and more than these,
    The seven-fold vision of the Florentine,
    And grave-browed Milton's solemn harmonies.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Oscar Fingal O'Flahertie Wills Wilde, titled "Amor Intellectualis", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
